title: leontodon taraxacum var. palustris
intro: Leontodon taraxacum var. palustris, also known as marsh dandelion or marsh hawk's beard, is a rare and lesser-known variant of the common dandelion plant, characterized by its ability to thrive in marshy and wet environments. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues due to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Leontodon taraxacum var. palustris include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve liver function, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
